Digital Audio Samples

In order to play digital audio sample data, it must be copied to an internal JSyn resource called a "Sample". More...

Functions

CSynToken CSyn_CreateSample (CSynContext context, uint32 numFrames, uint32 numChannels)
 Create a sample resource with the given number of frames and channels.

CSynErr CSyn_WriteSample (CSynContext context, CSynToken sample, uint32 startFrame, short *address, uint32 numFrames)
 Write 16 bit data from the application memory space into the internal JSyn Sample memory.

CSynErr CSyn_ReadSample (CSynContext context, CSynToken sample, uint32 startFrame, short *address, uint32 numFrames)
 Read 16 bit data from the internal JSyn Sample memory to the application memory space.

Detailed Description

In order to play digital audio sample data, it must be copied to an internal JSyn resource called a "Sample".

Samples can have either 1 or 2 channels. A "frame" is one number for a mono sample, and two numbers for a stereo Sample. A sample could be played using a sample reader such as CSYN_UNIT_Sample_Read16V1. Sample data can only be played sequentially from one frame to the next.

Function Documentation

CSynToken CSyn_CreateSample (  CSynContext  context,
uint32  numFrames,
uint32  numChannels
) 
 

Create a sample resource with the given number of frames and channels.

The value of numFrames must be positive and is limited by the amount of available memory. The parameter numChannels must be 1 or 2. Returns a negative number on error, or a token that can then be used with other sample related functions.

CSynErr CSyn_ReadSample (  CSynContext  context,
CSynToken  sample,
uint32  startFrame,
short *  address,
uint32  numFrames
) 
 

Read 16 bit data from the internal JSyn Sample memory to the application memory space.

The parameter startFrame is the first frame in the Sample that will be read.

CSynErr CSyn_WriteSample (  CSynContext  context,
CSynToken  sample,
uint32  startFrame,
short *  address,
uint32  numFrames
) 
 

Write 16 bit data from the application memory space into the internal JSyn Sample memory.

The parameter startFrame is the first frame in the Sample that will be written.
